question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 9.00% on 2026-01-14 for the Metaculus question "Will the Democrats control the House of Representatives at any point before Jan 3, 2027?"?
summary: Meta-prediction: Will the CP on 'Democrats control House before Jan 3 2027' be strictly above 9.00% on Jan 14, 2026? The CP is currently exactly 9.0% (as of Jan 3, 2026) and has NOT exceeded 9% in the last 3 months, oscillating between 6-9% with a strong long-term downward trend (25% → 9% over 10 months). With only 22 forecasters, there's some volatility risk, but the threshold hasn't been breached in months and there's no catalyst to push it higher. Forecast: ~30% YES.

factors.description: CP has not exceeded 9% in the last 3 months (10+ readings all at or below 9%)
Strong long-term downward trend: 25% (Mar 2025) → 9% (Jan 2026), consistent decay as underlying event becomes less likely
CP currently exactly AT threshold (9.0%), so even a tiny upward perturbation would trigger YES
Only 22 forecasters - small pool creates meaningful random perturbation risk from a single new forecast
No known political catalyst (special elections, resignations) to shift sentiment on Democrats gaining House control
Time passage naturally pushes CP lower as fewer months remain for Democrats to gain majority before Jan 2027

reasoning:

Key reasoning for this meta-prediction:

- **Current CP is exactly at the threshold** (9.0% as of Jan 3, 2026). The question asks if it will be *strictly above* 9.00% on Jan 14.
- **3-month track record strongly favors NO**: The CP has not exceeded 9% in any of the ~10 readings since late October 2025. It has oscillated between 6-9%, predominantly at 8%.
- **Long-term downward trend**: CP has declined from 25% to 9% over 10 months, reflecting the underlying reality that Democrats gaining House control becomes less likely as time passes.
- **Small forecaster pool (22)** introduces some volatility — one new forecast could perturb the CP above 9%, which is the main source of YES probability.
- **No political catalyst**: No known imminent special elections or resignations that would shift forecaster sentiment upward.

The 30% accounts for the non-trivial risk that random perturbation from the small forecaster pool pushes CP marginally above 9%, while reflecting the strong base case that the CP remains at or below 9%.
sources_consulted: US House of Representatives special election resignation vacancy 2026
